summary: Initial camera scaffold only; no cutscene system exists, but weapon feedback now depends on shared camera-local view-pose math, pitch-corrected shot distance math, and hold-fire FOV/weapon-centering state.
observations: No camera modes, viewmodel framing, or cutscene system exists yet.
Weapon muzzle, tracer, and wall-impact positions are derived from src/game/weapons/weaponViewPose.ts.
Pitched shot tracer distance is corrected by src/game/weapons/weaponShotMath.ts because level wall raycasts operate in flat X/Z space.
No deterministic pitch-up or pitch-down debug camera poses exist yet.
Holding fire blends camera FOV from 70 to 62 degrees and blends each weapon from its hip pose toward a centered aim pose.
snapshot.weapon.aimBlend and snapshot.weapon.cameraFovDegrees expose the current aim camera state.
Fire-button drag can adjust yaw/pitch at reduced sensitivity while the same pointer is held for fire.
decisions: Input state must be restored after cutscenes.
Camera shake and FOV shifts must remain readable on mobile.
Gameplay camera changes must preserve or explicitly suspend weapon shot-effect pose alignment.
Future aim/FOV/recoil camera changes must preserve the flat-raycast to camera-space shot-distance conversion.
Treat hold-fire as the current aim camera mode until a formal camera state machine exists.
Any future camera mode must restore base FOV when held fire, reload, or cutscene state ends.
caughtIssues: Pointer-drag screenshot QA can approximate pitch-up/down firing, but deterministic debug look poses are needed for stable visual regression.
Held-fire centering is debug-state tested but not screenshot-regressed for each weapon silhouette.
risks: Camera and viewmodel decisions can break touch controls and HUD readability if reviewed late.
